macro_events.event_summary: Markets are rallying on news of a tentative U.S.-Iran agreement (likely involving sanctions relief / nuclear de-escalation), though the deal has not yet been signed.
primary_drivers: Removal of Middle East geopolitical risk premium in oil
Potential return of Iranian crude (~1-1.5 mbpd) to global supply, pressuring prices lower
Lower energy costs flowing through to consumer/industrial margins and easing inflation expectations

beneficiaries.rationale: Jet fuel is ~25-30% of operating costs at Southwest; a sustained drop in crude from Iranian supply return directly expands airline margins. LUV is the most fuel-cost-sensitive major US carrier given unhedged exposure in recent years.
Same jet fuel margin tailwind as LUV; Delta has large international route exposure where lower fuel + reduced Middle East airspace risk premium compounds the benefit.

Bunker fuel is the single largest variable cost for cruise lines; lower crude directly drops to EBITDA. Cruise stocks also rally on de-escalation as it removes a tail risk to discretionary travel demand.
Ethane/naphtha feedstock costs track crude; lower oil compresses input costs for commodity chemicals producers. Dow is a high-beta play on this spread.

Lower diesel/gasoline prices reduce trucking and last-mile distribution costs across Walmart's domestic supply chain, and lower pump prices free up low-income consumer wallet share — Walmart's core demographic.

losers.rationale: Integrated major with high leverage to Brent crude; return of ~1+ mbpd Iranian barrels would pressure global oil prices and 2026 upstream earnings. Refining margins partially offset but net negative.
Pure-play US shale producer with no integrated downstream hedge; among the most oil-price-sensitive large caps. Every $5 drop in WTI materially hits FCF and debt paydown pace.
Permian pure-play with high oil cut and breakevens that get squeezed as crude falls; dividend variable component shrinks with lower realizations.
Middle East de-escalation reduces near-term urgency for missile defense (THAAD, PAC-3) restocking and foreign military sales to Gulf states — Lockheed is the most exposed prime to this thesis.
Raytheon missiles & defense segment benefits from Middle East tension (Patriot, SM-series, Tomahawk reorders); peace deal moderates the FMS pipeline narrative.
Oilfield services revenue is a derivative of E&P capex, which falls with lower oil prices; Halliburton is North America levered where shale cuts hit fastest.

headlines: Markets cheer U.S.-Iran agreement, but some investors caution deal is yet to be signed
